title: lamium eriocephalum subsp. glandulosidens
intro: Lamium eriocephalum subsp. glandulosidens, also known as Spotted Deadnettle, is a flowering plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Lamium eriocephalum subsp. glandulosidens include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving spasms. The herbal preparations of Lamium eriocephalum subsp. glandulosidens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
